sourcesContent:
import React from 'react'; const projects = [ { num: '01', title: 'Student Task Manager', desc: 'A full-stack web application for students to manage assignments, deadlines, and study schedules. Features user authentication, CRUD operations, and a responsive dashboard.', tech: ['React.js', 'Node.js', 'Express', 'MongoDB'], github: 'https://github.com/', live: '#', bg: 'linear-gradient(135deg, rgba(124,58,237,0.3), rgba(0,212,255,0.15))', icon: '📋', }, { num: '02', title: 'Weather Dashboard', desc: 'A React-based weather application that fetches real-time data from OpenWeatherMap API. Users can search any city and view temperature, humidity, and 5-day forecast.', tech: ['React.js', 'REST API', 'CSS3', 'Axios'], github: 'https://github.com/', live: '#', bg: 'linear-gradient(135deg, rgba(0,212,255,0.25), rgba(6,214,160,0.15))', icon: '🌦️', }, { num: '03', title: 'E-Commerce Product Page', desc: 'A responsive product listing page with cart functionality built in React. Implements state management with React hooks and features filter, sort, and add-to-cart actions.', tech: ['React.js', 'Context API', 'CSS Modules', 'JavaScript'], github: 'https://github.com/', live: '#', bg: 'linear-gradient(135deg, rgba(6,214,160,0.25), rgba(124,58,237,0.15))', icon: '🛒', }, ]; function Projects() { return (
What I've Built

Projects

{projects.map((project) => (
{project.icon}
{project.num}

{project.title}

{project.desc}

{project.tech.map((t) => ( {t} ))}
↗ GitHub ↗ Live Demo
))}
); } export default Projects;
